---
title: "Generate Plots"
format: 
  typst: default
  html: default
fig-width: 7
fig-height: 3
keep-typ: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(thematic)
library(ragg)
library(showtext)

posit_colors <- list(
  blue = "#447099",
  grey = "#404041",
  orange = "#EE6331",
  dark_blue_2 = "#213D4F",
  dark_blue_3 = "#17212B",
  light_blue_1 = "#D1DBE5",
  light_blue_2 = "#A2B8CB"
)

thematic_on(
  bg = "white",
  fg = posit_colors$grey,
  accent = posit_colors$blue,
  font = "Open Sans"
)

python <- read_csv(here("data", "python_package_downloads.csv"))
r <- read_csv(here("data", "r_package_downloads.csv"))
rstudio <- read_csv(here("data", "rstudio_os_downloads.csv"))
quarto <- read_csv(here("data", "quarto_downloads.csv"), col_types = cols(major_minor = col_character()))

plot_defaults <- list(
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_number(scale_cut = scales::cut_short_scale())
  ),
  xlab(""),
  ylab("Cumulative Downloads"),
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#EDF0F5"),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 8.5, 5.5, 5.5)
  )
)

growth_plot <- function(data, y = downloads) {
  data |>
    ggplot(aes(x = date, y = cumsum(as.numeric(downloads)))) +
    geom_line(color = posit_colors$blue, linewidth = 0.75) +
    scale_x_date(labels = scales::label_date_short()) +
    plot_defaults
}
```

## Open source packages

```{r}
#| label: quarto
quarto |> 
  mutate(major_minor = if_else(major_minor == "1.10", "1.10\nPre-release", as.character(major_minor)),
  major_minor = factor(major_minor, levels =major_minor)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(major_minor, cumsum(downloads))) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = posit_colors$blue, linewidth = 0.75) +  
  plot_defaults
```

```{r}
#| label: shiny
#| fig-height: 2.4
python |> 
  filter(package == "shiny") |> 
  growth_plot() + ggtitle("Shiny for Python")
r |> filter(package == "shiny") |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("Shiny for R")
```

```{r}
#| label: gt
python |> filter(package == "great-tables") |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("great_tables")
r |> filter(package == "gt") |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("gt")
```

```{r}
#| label: vetiver
python |> filter(package == "vetiver") |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("vetiver for Python")
r |> filter(package == "vetiver", year(date) > 2021) |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("vetiver for R")
```

```{r}
#| label: plotnine
python |> filter(package == "plotnine") |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("Plotnine")
```

```{r}
#| label: siuba
python |> filter(package == "siuba") |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("Siuba")
```

```{r}
#| label: pins
python |> filter(package == "pins") |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("pins for Python")
r |> filter(package == "pins", year(date) > 2021) |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("pins for R")
```

```{r}
#| label: rstudio
rstudio |> growth_plot() + ggtitle("RStudio")
```

```{r}
#| label: tidyverse
r |> 
  filter(project == "tidyverse") |> 
  group_by(date) |> 
  summarise(downloads = sum(downloads)) |> 
  growth_plot() + 
  ggtitle("Tidyverse")
```

```{r}
#| label: tidymodels
r |> 
  filter(project == "tidymodels") |> 
  group_by(date) |> 
  summarise(downloads = sum(downloads)) |> 
  growth_plot() + 
  ggtitle("Tidymodels")
```

```{r}
#| label: connectivity
r |> 
  filter(project == "connectivity") |> 
  group_by(date) |> 
  summarise(downloads = sum(downloads)) |> 
  growth_plot() + 
  ggtitle("R Connectivity")
```

```{r}
#| label: rlibs
r |> 
  filter(project == "r-lib") |> 
  group_by(date) |> 
  summarise(downloads = sum(downloads)) |> 
  growth_plot() + 
  ggtitle("R Libs")
```

## Carbon Offsets

```{r}
#| fig-height: 3
#| label: emissions
emissions <- tribble(
  ~emission_source,                 ~`2023`, ~`2024`, ~`2025`,
  "SaaS Software & Cloud Computing", 321.80,  300.34,  325.60,
  "Business Travel",                  13.91,   51.65,  168.97,
  "Shared Office Commuting",          17.70,   17.70,   17.70,
  "Boston Office Electricity",         3.97,    4.32,    3.80
  ) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -emission_source, 
    names_to = "year", 
    values_to = "emissions"
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    emission_source = factor(
      emission_source, 
      levels = rev(c(
        "SaaS Software & Cloud Computing",
        "Business Travel",
        "Shared Office Commuting",
        "Boston Office Electricity"
      ))
    )
  )

# Luminance-stepped, color-blind-safe palette for the stacked bars
emission_colors <- c(
  "SaaS Software & Cloud Computing" = "#D1DBE5",  # light-blue-1
  "Business Travel"                 = "#94BDBF",  # light teal
  "Shared Office Commuting"         = "#EE6331",  # orange
  "Boston Office Electricity"       = "#707073"   # light-gray-3
)

emissions  |>
  ggplot(aes(year, emissions, fill = emission_source)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("") + ylab("CO₂e (metric tons)") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Source",
    values = emission_colors,
    labels = scales::label_wrap(18)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.key.spacing.y = unit(6, "pt"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#EDF0F5"),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 8.5, 5.5, 5.5)
  )
```
